profiles.name: Senior Business and Investment Advisor,
profiles.title: The Pacific Mining Company
profiles.bio: Adaptable and dependable professional with excellent interpersonal and time management skills, ability to work in teams and communicate effectively. Practical experience in the mining industry, and consulting and advising public and private organizations within the sector in various Latin American countries. Extensive know-how in the banking sector and academia, having lectured on topics such as mineral economics, international trade, and competitiveness. With constant focus on up-skilling and tackling challenges in new roles, completed diverse postgraduate studies from renowned institutions in The United Kingdom, Australia, and Chile. Key areas of learning were leadership, project evaluation, and financial management. With a keen interest to contribute to the public discussion surrounding the extractive industry's most pressing issues and opportunities in the medium and long term, authored book “Chile, Mining in The 21st Century” in 2017. The book deals with climate change and energy critical elements, and has garnered exceptional reviews from experts of the industry.
career_highlights: Analyzed investment projects over US$ 500 million for the Pacific Mining Company. Evaluated potential acquisitions worth around US$ 2 billion, including detailed assessments of market capitalization, assets, and financial data.

Advised public and private organizations on mining policy goals and business development, including the Chilean Copper Commission, the Center for Copper and Mining Studies, and the Namibian embassy for South America.

Co-founder of MIINt (Intelligent Mining), a startup initially focused on strengthening a capital market for local medium-sized mining. Over time, it pivoted to a focus on developing cutting-edge technology tailored to the mining industry. Fernando Acosta Senior Business and Investment Advisor, The Pacific Mining Company

profiles.name: Business Development Director, JiBe, Israel
profiles.title: Change Leader l New Ventures l Corporate Entrepreneur
profiles.bio: With two decades of experience from family-run businesses & startups to Government bodies & large multinationals, Neil's background in the maritime, industrial, tech & education industries has mapped diverse geographies, demonstrating a robust career path. His key insights around Maritime Operations, Business Development, New Ventures, Change & Digital Transformation have enabled him to leverage his creative & ambidextrous leadership skills to impact businesses. Sailing internationally with multicultural teams early on in his career, often under extreme conditions, laid the foundations for his resilience while navigating adversities. His diverse experiences have helped to learn the art of unlearning and managing change during uncertainties, whether in a ship's engine room,implementing new technologies or reporting to C-suite executives to transform sales and marketing functions. His passion for helping professionals motivated him to launch Indo Pacific Marine Services, a marine skills development venture in New Zealand. Over the seven years he ran the venture, Neil collaborated with C-suite partners, government agencies and institutions to upskill over 1000 marine professionals and counselled 5000+ students. Intrigued about self-actualization, he actively draws inspiration from Gardner's Multiple Intelligence Theory and Vedic astrology research to coach true inner potential. As the race to decarbonize the maritime industry is now underway, Neil is currently developing a climate tech venture focused on reducing Greenhouse gas emissions from merchant ships.
career_highlights: Instituted an exclusive marine skills development venture with New Zealand training providers and drove revenue greater than USD 8M by recruiting 1000+ maritime professionals.

Spearheaded organizational change & digital transformation in traditional family businesses balancing democratic with autocratic practices & acting as a catalyst of behavioural change across senior management teams.

Managed several crises in international marine operations leading multicultural teams, including an intricate takeover as Owner's Representative of a large merchant ship from Mitsui O.S.K. Lines, Japan. Neil Advani Business Development Director, JiBe, Israel Change Leader l New Ventures l Corporate Entrepreneur

profiles.name: George Berezhnov

profiles.bio: Authentic leader with a passion for driving innovation and new ventures in established companies. Trusted member of leadership team with track record of delivering human-centric solutions that unlock new sources of value, redefine customer experience and help people lead happier and healthier lives. With an academic background in Robotics and Artificial Intelligence, combined with two business degrees from the University of Warwick and London Business School, George brings a rare combination of technical expertise and business acumen to the table. Throughout his 17+ year career at Jaguar Land Rover and Johnson & Johnson, George has gained cross-functional exposure across product marketing, sales, digital, market access, strategy, change, and program management. His experience spans both the automotive and healthcare sectors, with a particular focus on driving transformative change in emerging markets. An articulate communicator and skilled change leader, George has a natural ability to set vision, interact effectively with people of diverse cultures and backgrounds and lead teams through change. As a director of innovation and new ventures George has helped Johnson & Johnson to imagine, build, and scale a new innovation arm to deliver cutting-edge healthcare solutions while also developing the critical internal capabilities required for accelerating long-term growth. George is a One Young World ambassador and has led multiple pro- bono consulting assignments with NGOs to drive positive change.
career_highlights: Established innovation unit from the ground-up to drive continuous transformation. Designed corporate innovation strategy, launched internal accelerator. Built new partnership ecosystem and pipeline of digital services valued at $100 million.

Led development of new business strategy for pharmaceutical business of Johnson & Johnson in Russia & CIS cluster. Enabled 18.3% annual growth during 2016-2021 and launched the company to #1 pharmaceutical company in prescription market.

Designed and implemented multiple go-to-market strategies, healthcare solutions and innovative affordability programs in public and private sectors. Enabled unique cross-industry partnerships and expanded access to innovative care for over 1500 patients. George Berezhnov Innovation & New Ventures Director, Johnson & Johnson

profiles.name: Bruna Bueno
profiles.title: Chief of Staff of the CTO, VTEX
profiles.bio: An engineer with 12+ years of experience working on projects in diversified industries and segments, ranging from strategic planning, preparing business plans to implementing organizational systems. With critical thinking and problem-solving skills that I acquired as a consultant, I learned how to build bridges between teams, translating the executives' vision into tangible action plans, and delivering results. I'm passionate about building things from scratch and entrepreneurship: In 2011, after winning a Startup Weekend prize, I led the development of a marketplace booking system focussed on beauty salon services. The solution was successfully implemented in three salons in Rio de Janeiro. In my last role at VTEX, I was an active member of the leadership team that led the company on a hyper-growth trajectory. I led the disruption of HR function - from being detached from the business to being an active ally in the decision-making process. We thrived in influencing the business and promoted collaboration without creating an operational arm. Now as Chief of Staff, I manage CTO's workload by foreseeing and resolving issues, and advising on crucial decisions. I also work actively on the planning and execution of strategic projects, such as Tech Strategic Planning and Budgeting exercise.
career_highlights: Reformulated People Operations team successfully raising the performance of the established level of service with Workday implementation, minimized labor risk, and reducing cost of operation with an estimated savings of +USD2MM in one year.

As Director of People Operations, I lead a 35+ team, overseeing $6MM+ monthly global payrolls and HR processes for 1700+ employees across 17 countries. Alongside with the CFO, I conducted the HR component of our NYSE IPO.

I established and directed the Talent Acquisition department, scaling the company from 370 to 1200+ employees. I instituted a robust hiring process, tools and culture, with a high connection to the business. Bruna Bueno Chief of Staff of the CTO, VTEX

profiles.name: Co-Business Leader | Group Managing Director,
profiles.title: NewGlobe Education | ex. Country Director, Andela
profiles.bio: Omowale is a passionate, fearless and trusted business leader. As a people developer she has a track record of successfully leading large teams, projects and country operations to deliver business results. With a career that spans serving on the Executive Team of three global companies as well as founding and scaling her own company, she combines execution-focus with social intelligence. As Group Managing Director (Nigeria), NewGlobe Education, Omowale was responsible for ensuring millions of children in sub- Saharan Africa received life-changing education. Through multi-year technical partnerships with state governments she managed and transformed their public schools into globally competitive ones. Previously as Vice President, Global Operations at Andela (a tech start- up), she centralized a distributed team to provide an effective work environment for a fully remote workforce of 1000+ engineers across six countries. Prior to that as Country Director (Nigeria and Ghana) she managed circa 800 employees, delivering software engineering services to clients worldwide. Omowale founded and managed Strictly Babes Ltd (SBL) where she conceptualised, established, and scaled the first maternity-only retailer in Nigeria. She still acts as a Non-Executive Director. She was an Executive (Senior Manager) at Accenture, where for eleven years she led complex and challenging projects for strategic clients in both the public and private sectors of Nigeria. She also, temporarily, acted as Head of Human Resources in addition to her consulting responsibilities.
career_highlights: Drove market expansion of NewGlobe within 18months by supervising and collaborating with the partnerships team to add and renew State Programmes. This culminated in educating almost 1 million children in Nigerian public primary and secondary schools.

In Andela, converted decentralised operations to a centralised model, leading to a significant reduction in operating expenses. Concurrently handled the transition and stabilisation of a workforce of 1000+ Engineers to fully remote operations across 5 countries.

Doubled revenue of maternity retail company (SBL) by 2nd year, launching own-label products within the same period, winning a highly competitive national grant and scaling from a start-up to a multi-store brand. Gaurav has gained extensive multi-sector exposure, including consumer goods, pharmaceuticals, real estate, infrastructure, and technology. His strong research background and people skills have contributed to creating significant value not only for Blackstone's fund but also for its portfolio companies. As an investor and strategist, Gaurav has witnessed the positive social impact of business turnarounds, such as job creation, efficient resource redeployment, and the stabilisation of credit cycles in the economy. He is actively focusing on ESG principles to make a meaningful contribution to businesses and drive positive social and environmental change.

profiles.name: Dhliwayo
profiles.title: CEO, National Railways of Zimbabwe Contributory Pension Fund
profiles.bio: Prior to the Sloan programme, Patience had 26 years' experience, 16 of which were in the C-Suite of a large Zimbabwean pension fund. During her tenure with the pension fund, she mastered the art of prudently managing and preserving other people`s funds in a turbulent economic environment. Her experience spans financial reporting, financial management, treasury investments, real estate investments, equity, and bond markets investments as well as risk management and strategic management. As CEO of NRZCPF, she has managed assets of over USD400m, 30% of which are held in the UK. Thus, her experience spans both Zimbabwean and UK financial markets. She has successfully led teams and projects implementing NRZCPF`s digitization, setting up a Real estate investment trust and utilizing the Fund`s real estate portfolio to generate solar energy from rooftop systems. Patience's greatest achievement was the ability to influence policies at macro level that facilitated the reinstatement of members` pension savings which were wiped out by two separate periods of hyperinflation in two decades. Post Sloan, Patience is looking to catapult her career to the broader global market by leveraging her experience in financial services and particularly her high conscientiousness in managing other people's funds.
career_highlights: Managed a +USD400m pension fund and preserved its assets through hyperinflationary periods in Zimbabwe. Being the only pension fund in Zimbabwe to consistently pay benefits in a timely manner as well as restoration of pension values.
Established the first Real Estate Investment Trust in Zimbabwe in 2021 with seed capital of USD20m.
Established the first rooftop solar project in Zimbabwe with capacity of 10MW and cost at USD10m in 2022. Patience Bekezela Dhliwayo CEO, National Railways of Zimbabwe Contributory Pension Fund

profiles.name: Tomas Drucker
profiles.title: Senior Executive | CEO | Minister
profiles.bio: Tomas is an accomplished and innovative leader with significant achievements in various industries, including entrepreneurship, big corporate management, and government. As the former Minister of Health in the Slovak Republic, Tomas made substantial progress in the healthcare sector through innovative programs and initiatives as evaluated by the EU Commission. During the EU presidency in 2016, Tomas Drucker served as the Chairman of the Council of EU (EPSCO). In his role as Chairman and CEO of Slovak Post, he led a turnaround through a transformative change management program, revolutionizing the organization and earning recognition as “The Most Respected CEO in Slovakia” by Forbes and PwC in 2016. With a Master of Science in Applied Informatics and a Master of Law, Drucker's skills and varied experience have allowed him to make meaningful contributions across different industries. “Throughout my career, I've been fortunate enough to work in both private and public sectors, obtaining valuable experience as a consultant, CEO, and Minister of Health. This journey has emphasized the vital role of powerful leaders in driving transformation within business and public policies. My diverse roles, including entrepreneurship in business consulting, leading a major company as CEO, and serving as Minister, have equipped me with essential transferable skills that are highly applicable in other domains”.
career_highlights: Tomas spearheaded a healthcare change management program. He implemented eHealth, first DRG, and a new drug categorization system. Additionally, he committed to the Value for Money project, effectively reducing healthcare costs and increasing value.

He successfully led a transformative change management program at Slovak Post, turning it into a multifaceted service provider. He guided 14,000 employees across 1,500 branches to effectively execute turnaround program.

He founded a consulting company in Central Europe. He participated in the merger of health insurance companies in Slovakia, the implementation of the euro currency, the optimization of different companies in various industries and other projects. Tomas Drucker Senior Executive | CEO | Minister

profiles.name: Dullat
profiles.title: Colonel, Indian Army
profiles.bio: Anupinder has had an exceptional military career spanning two decades across four continents. He has repeatedly set new benchmarks of performance and achievement in a diverse range of military activities and skills including weapons technology, combat vehicles, management, leadership, training and development, supply chain & logistics and commando skills. He is a certified trainer on seven military domains, with a distinction in two. He has commanded teams ranging from 5 to 800 persons and delivered results in challenging operational and leadership scenarios across the globe. During the three years of his exceptional command of an armoured infantry battalion, his team garnered more honours and awards than they had in the 37 years since they were founded. Anupinder's team was also selected to be part of the Indian Army's United Nations Contingent in South Sudan. In his last role as the Director of Strategy & Operations at a Corps Headquarter, with 45,000 troops under command, he prepared a blueprint for force restructuring which has been adopted for pan Army implementation. Using his exceptional stakeholder management skills, he was able to secure 650% additional for developing critical military infrastructure in extremely rugged terrain along the International Border. He even conceived a novel model of civil-military cooperation for construction of military infrastructure in remote mountainous areas along the international borders and implemented the pilot project by involving stakeholders from civilian organisations, military units and government agencies. A well rounded personality, Anupinder has a keen interest in sports, travelling, music and motorcycling. He has travelled to over 25 countries, has played five sports at a competitive level on the Army circuit and plays various musical instruments. He is open to Geographical relocation.
career_highlights: Led Indian Army delegation on a deployment along with the Indian Navy to Vietnam. Selected to be a United Nations Peacekeeper in the Democratic Republic of Congo from 2007 to 2008.

Selected, following a competitive process involving over 1,500 officers, to represent the Indian Army at the Defence Academy of the UK and attend the Advanced Command and Staff Course (ACSC) from 2013-2014 alongside military professionals from c. 50 countries.

For his exceptional performance in designing & delivering training to military professionals from 6 countries, he was awarded a Commendation by the General-Officer-Commanding-in-Chief of the Western Army Command, India. Anupinder Singh Dullat Colonel, Indian Army

profiles.name: Joseph Eboigbe
profiles.title: Chartered Accountant | Development Practioner
profiles.bio: Over the course of his career, Joseph achieved a diverse set of accomplishments in Government, Financial Services and Professional Services. He commenced his career in Government as Special Adviser for Budget & Economic Planning responsible for providing expert advice on the formulation and execution of socio-economic policies and plans to deliver sustainable and inclusive growth. He coordinated the budgeting process for the State Government and its Local Government Councils. He was subsequently appointed Commissioner for Finance, responsible for fiscal policy management, treasury and investment management (including infrastructure project development/finance), process and systems integrity, and financial reporting. During his tenure as Commissioner, he served as member of Nigeria's National Economic Council (NEC), and the Federation Account Allocation Committee (FAAC). Before his appointment at Edo State Government, Joseph entered a strategic management contract with Bankers Warehouse Plc., where he functioned as head of Strategy & Enterprise Development (Bankers Warehouse are the foremost Cash Logistics company in Nigeria licensed by the CBN to render physical currency management services). He had primary responsibility for coordinating planning & plan implementation, corporate performance management, business expansion and strategic marketing. Joseph led the West Africa consulting practice of Deloitte (with country operations in Nigeria, Ghana and Gambia). In this role, he had full responsibility for developing and implementing strategic plans covering: solutions and services, client retention and clientele growth, people and talent management, P&L management, and practice risk management.
career_highlights: Spent 12 years in senior management roles at Deloitte (including 8 years as Partner). Led a multinational and multicultural team, delivering advisory services to clients in the UK and across West Africa.

Led the Ministry of Finance (Edo State Govt. Nigeria), for 4 years and provided quality leadership to a team of economists and accountants. Together, we fostered a stable macroeconomic environment and attained fiscal policy targets for productivity, GDP and employment growth.

Handled extensive funding negotiations with executive level officers in leading National and International Development Finance Institutions (including the World Bank and Central Bank of Nigeria). Joseph Eboigbe Chartered Accountant | Development Practioner

Throughout his career, Eran has been at the forefront of numerous transformative initiatives, utilizing sports and culture as a powerful engine to inspire the youth, unify communities, and address societal issues such as inequality, violence and racism. His distinctive approach, marked by fostering innovative and sustainable collaborations, has proven successful across diverse sectors that span the private, public, and non-profit organizations. Eran's multifaceted skill-set converged when he took the lead in launching the first Sports Fund for Youth in Israel, which provided social, emotional and financial support to organizations during the significant upheaval caused by the covid-19 crisis. Eran's strategic acumen honed from experiences ranging from coaching at elite talent levels, consulting, serving as CEO and government Chief of Staff, has fostered a unique leadership style. This distinctive approach empowers him to adeptly navigate complex challenges and engage diverse stakeholders, including state leaders, government ministries, and top executives of major corporations.

As CEO, transformed Value Sport from a small, local non-profit into a strong leading government organization in sport and education in Israel, impacting 3,000 coaches, 100,000 youth, pro sports teams, and national governance.

He led the corporate finance, strategy, acquisitions, investment management and governance functions of the subsidiaries, trade investments, partnerships and joint ventures in GCC, Sub Sahara Africa, Europe and Far East across multiple sectors such as mineral concessions, mining, banks, fintech, trading and industrial agriculture. Previously, Farakh was headhunted by HavenVest Private Equity [formerly HSBC Private Equity ME], a DIFC - UAE regulated operator of 3 PE funds with committed capital of $700m, to serve as the CFO of its 3 portfolio investments in the manufacturing and construction trading industries. For over 3 years in this role, he was responsible for 50 staff across finance, procurement and treasury functions and served as a ‘turn around advisor' with direct reporting to the PE Board.

Momo has a proven track record in industry - occupying dual roles as a CEO and Head of Investment Management , he is responsible for the implementation of investment policy and strategy. With international experience and local expert knowledge, he brings a different perspective in terms of leadership and investment management to the organization and beyond. Once in Madagascar, in 2010, he was surprised to learn that all the toilet paper in his country was being imported. Sensing an opportunity, set out on a journey of creating Société de Production d'Articles Hygiéniques (S.P.A.H), the only tissue paper mill in Madagascar and the Indian Ocean region to use recycled paper as raw materials. Despite no background in the industry, he built a successful business from a scratch. He participated in all the important stages of the business which included sourcing a supplier for the production line from China, securing funding, importing required materials and machinery, installation and commissioning of the production line, staff recruitment and training, creating and growing the brand, and establishing a distribution network. Within 5 years, his brainchild became the industry leader within the country, with a market share of 45%, and an EBITDA margin of 34% . More importantly, they recycled over c.1,500 tons of waste paper per year and provided employment to 95 people. He successfully exited from the company in December 2022 to pursue the Sloan programme at London Business School. Nirina was also actively involved in the development of the private sector in Madagascar as President of Jeune Patronat de Madagascar and the Vice-President of the Groupement des Entreprises de Madagascar which is the largest employers' organisation of Madagascar accounting for 45% of national added value and 77% of all taxes.
